Once upon a time, there was a mom and a dad and they had a little girl. The mom and dad were busy, so they asked the little girl to do an important job. 

"We need you to tie this knot," said the dad. He handed her a rope and showed her how to tie it up.

The little girl was excited about her job and began to tie the rope into a knot. But it was a bit tricky.

The dad saw that she was having trouble and said, "It's ok if you need help. We can do it together."

So the dad and the little girl worked together and eventually were able to tie the knot. Everyone was so proud of the little girl and her accomplishment.

The mom and dad thanked the little girl for being such a big help and said, "We couldn't have done it without you. You are very important and include."

The little girl felt so happy and smiled. She couldn't believe she had done such an important job and helped her mom and dad!

Lily was nervous. She had to go to the dentist today. She did not like the dentist. He made loud noises and poked her teeth. She wished she could shrink and hide under her pillow.

"Come on, Lily, it's time to go," her mom said. She held Lily's hand and took her to the car. Lily felt her tummy hurt. She did not want to go.

"Mom, can I stay home?" Lily asked. She looked at her mom with big eyes.

"No, sweetie, you have to go. The dentist will help you keep your teeth healthy and clean. It won't hurt, I promise," her mom said. She smiled and kissed Lily's cheek.

Lily sighed. She knew her mom was right, but she was still scared. She hugged her pillow in the car. She hoped the dentist would be nice and gentle.

When they got to the dentist's office, Lily saw a big sign that said "Welcome, Lily!" She also saw a picture of a smiling tooth. She felt a little better. Maybe the dentist was not so bad.

"Hello, Lily, I'm Dr. Lee. I'm happy to see you today," the dentist said. He wore a white coat and a mask. He looked friendly. He showed Lily his tools and explained what they did. He said they were like toys for teeth.

Lily listened and nodded. She felt more curious than nervous. She let the dentist look at her teeth and clean them. He said she had beautiful teeth and gave her a sticker and a toothbrush.

"Thank you, Dr. Lee," Lily said. She smiled and showed her teeth.

"You're very welcome, Lily. You were a brave and good patient. I hope to see you again soon," Dr. Lee said. He waved and said goodbye.

Lily felt proud and happy. She had gone to the dentist and it was not so bad. She had not shrunk or hidden under her pillow. She had faced her fear and learned something new. She hugged her mom and thanked her for taking her to the dentist. She was not nervous anymore. She was ready for a new adventure.

One day, he saw a friendly cat playing nearby. The friendly cat said to the peanut, â€œWill you come and play with me?â€ The peanut thought about it for a bit, but then he remembered how lazy he was.

But then, the friendly cat made a promise. The friendly cat said, â€œIf you come and play with me, I will give you a delicious treat.â€ That sounded very tempting to the peanut! He decided to come and play with the friendly cat.

When they were done playing, the friendly cat kept his promise and gave the peanut a delicious treat â€“ yummy peanutes! The lazy peanut was so excited and he ate the peanuts with a big smile. They both had a great time!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy had a big shelf in his room where he kept his toys. One day, Timmy was looking for his favorite toy car and realized it was not on the shelf. He looked everywhere but could not find it.

Suddenly, Timmy noticed something weird on the shelf. It was a funny looking toy that he had never seen before. He picked it up and played with it for a while, but it was not as fun as his favorite toy car.

Later that day, Timmy's mom found his favorite toy car under his bed. Timmy was happy to have it back and put it back on the shelf with all his other toys. He realized that sometimes things can be in weird places and it's important to look carefully.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mommy asked her if she wanted to help make pancakes for breakfast. Lily was very excited and said, "Yes, please!"

As they were making the pancakes, Lily thought of an original idea. She said to her mommy, "What if we put blueberries in the pancakes?" Her mommy thought that was a great idea and they added the blueberries to the pancake batter.

When the pancakes were done, Lily's mommy asked her how she liked them. Lily said, "I think they're yummy!" Her mommy smiled and said, "I'm glad you like them. Your original idea made them even better."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play in her cozy house. One day, Lily was playing hide-and-seek with her teddy bear. She looked everywhere, but she couldn't find him. 

Lily searched under the bed, behind the curtains, and even in the closet. But her teddy bear was nowhere to be seen. Feeling sad, she sat down on the cushion and started to cry. 

Suddenly, Lily felt something tough under the cushion. She lifted it up and there was her teddy bear, smiling at her! Lily was so happy to have found her teddy bear and hugged him tight. From that day on, she made sure to always check under the cushion when she was looking for something.

Lila and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big pile of wood. They want to make something with the wood.

They ask their moms if they can use the wood. Their moms say yes, but be careful. Lila and Tom are happy. They start to move the wood. They find some nails and a hammer. They think of what they can make.

They decide to make a house. They use the nails and the hammer to join the wood. They make a floor, a wall, a door, and a roof. They work hard and have fun. They are clever.

When they finish, they show their moms their house. Their moms are proud. They say it is a very good house. They say Lila and Tom are very clever. They say they are also very kind, because they share the wood.

Lila and Tom smile. They are happy. They invite their moms to come inside their house. They say they made the house to unite their families. They say they love their moms. They hug their moms. They are very clever and very kind.

One day, Timmy had a really busy day. He was playing, eating lunch and taking a nap all at the same time. He went to his bedroom to take a nap and had put his fork under his pillow. But just as he was about to fall asleep he heard a noise. He didn't know what it was, and he was very scared. He looked everywhere but he couldn't see anything.

Then he remembered his fork. He got it out from under the pillow and tried to find the hidden noise. He pressed it against the wall and heard a scratching sound from the other side. He felt brave and decided to investigate.

When he uncovered the wall, he saw a little mouse that was just as scared as he was! They both were too scared to move for a couple of seconds, but then the mouse saw how nice Timmy was and started to play with the fork. After playing together for a while, they both got tired and took a nap together.

Since then, Timmy and the mouse have been inseparable friends. Every day they take long naps, play together, and enjoy each other's company.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Joe and Bob. Joe was very rich, but Bob was not. Joe had lots of toys, but Bob did not. 

One day, Joe said to Bob, "let's go play!" So they both got up and went outside to play together. After a while they got very thirsty, so Joe said, "Let's go get something to drink!" 

They went to the store and Joe bought them both some drinks. Bob's drink was very cheap, but Joe's drink was very expensive. He was so rich that he could buy anything he wanted. 

The two friends sat down and had a drink together. They were so happy and they talked and laughed as they drank. When they were done, they said goodbye and went home. 

The end.

Once upon a time, there were two best friends named Jack and Sarah. They were playing together in the garden when Sarah found a cell. She jumped with excitement.

"Look Jack! I found a cell!" said Sarah.

Jack was surprised.

"Let me see it Sarah!" said Jack.

Sarah gave Jack the cell but he dropped it accidently.

"Oops!" said Jack.

Sarah was very angry and she slapped Jack. It was so high that Jack felt very surprised.

"Ouch! Why did you slap me?" said Jack.

Sarah was mad and she said, " You should be careful with the cell. It could get broken!"

Jack said, " I'm sorry Sarah. I won't do it again."

Sarah said, "OK. But it's time for dinner now. Let's go!"

Jack and Sarah went to dinner together. They were still best friends even though Sarah slapped Jack. They promised never to drop the cell again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside with her friends. One day, Lily's friend Emma came over to play. Emma said, "Let's go to the park!" Lily was excited and said, "Yes, let's go!"

At the park, they ran and played on the swings. Lily noticed that her arm muscle hurt. She said, "Ouch, my muscle is hurting." Emma said, "Maybe we should take a break and sit on the bench." 

Lily sat down on the bench and saw a bird flying in the sky. She said, "Look at that bird! It's so pretty." Emma replied, "Yes, it is. But the sky looks dull today." They sat and watched the bird fly until Lily's muscle felt better. Then they continued to play and have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lila. Lila had a very special toy that she trusted more than anything. It was a pretty bridge made of wood. 

One day, Lila wanted to find a new place to play with her bridge. She looked around and soon found a special spot. It was in the middle of the woods and there were lots of trees. 

Lila took her bridge and carefully crossed over to the special spot. Then, she looked around and found a stream of water. She found a perfect place to build her bridge. 

Lila and her bridge started to build the bridge together. Piece by piece, they connected the pieces until it was a big, strong bridge. Lila was very happy. She trusted the bridge and forever it stayed over the stream of water.

Tom and Lily were two rabbits who liked to play in the farm. They saw a big barrel near the barn and wanted to hop inside. They thought it would be fun to roll and bounce in the barrel.

"Come on, Lily, let's hop in the barrel!" Tom said.

"OK, Tom, but be careful. The barrel looks old and shaky," Lily said.

They hopped in the barrel and started to roll and bounce. They laughed and squeaked as they felt the wind in their fur. They did not see the farmer coming with his dog.

The farmer saw the barrel moving and heard the noise. He thought there was something inside that he could sell or eat. He grabbed the barrel and put it on his truck. He did not know there were two rabbits inside.

"Hey, what are you doing? Let us out!" Tom shouted.

"Help, help, we are trapped!" Lily cried.

But the farmer and his dog did not hear them. They drove away with the barrel. Tom and Lily were scared and sad. They did not know where they were going or what would happen to them. They wished they had not been so clumsy and curious. They wished they had stayed in their burrow. They missed their home and their friends. They hoped someone would save them, but no one did. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a toy bunny that she loved very much. One day, she accidentally tore off one of its ears. She was very sad because she loved her bunny just the way it was. 

Her mom saw how upset she was and told her not to worry. She said they would replace the bunny's ear with a new one. Lily didn't know what "replace" meant, but her mom explained that they would fix the bunny so it would look just like the original. 

Lily was so happy to hear that. She made a wish that the bunny would be fixed right away. And the next day, her mom surprised her with the bunny all fixed up with a new ear. Lily was overjoyed and hugged her bunny tight. She was so happy to have her favorite toy back to its original self.

Lily was a shy girl. She did not like to talk to new people. She liked to play with her dolls and her books. She had a big sister, Anna, who was very friendly. Anna liked to talk to everyone and make new friends.

One day, Anna had a birthday. She got many gifts from her family and friends. She got a bike, a dress, a puzzle, and a teddy bear. She was very happy and thanked everyone. Lily watched from the corner. She wanted to give Anna a gift too, but she did not know what to give. She had no money to buy anything.

She went to her room and looked around. She saw her favorite doll, Rosie. Rosie had long hair, a pink dress, and a smile. Lily loved Rosie very much. She hugged Rosie and whispered, "You are my best friend. But I want to make Anna happy too. Can I give you to her as a gift?"

Rosie did not answer, but Lily felt that Rosie understood. She wrapped Rosie in a scarf and put her in a box. She wrote "To Anna, from Lily" on the box. She took a deep breath and went to the living room. She saw Anna playing with her new bike. She walked slowly to her and said, "Anna, I have a gift for you too."

Anna looked at Lily and smiled. She put down her bike and took the box from Lily. She opened it and saw Rosie. She gasped and said, "Oh, Lily, this is Rosie! She is your favorite doll! You are giving her to me?"

Lily nodded and said, "Yes, I want you to have her. She is very nice and fun. She can be your friend too."

Anna hugged Lily and said, "Thank you, Lily, this is the best gift ever! You are very kind and brave. I love you and Rosie very much. Can we play with her together?"

Lily smiled and said, "Yes, we can. I love you too, Anna."

They played with Rosie and their other toys. Lily felt happy and proud. She was still shy, but she had become a little more confident. She had given Anna a gift from her heart.

Sam and Jen were twins who liked to go to the farm with their dad. They loved to see the animals and feed them carrots and apples. One day, they saw a new animal at the farm. It was a mule. A mule was like a horse, but with bigger ears and a louder voice.

"Can we pet the mule, dad?" Sam asked.

"Sure, but be careful. He is very strong and sometimes stubborn. You have to hold his halter gently and talk to him softly," dad said.

Sam and Jen took turns to hold the mule's halter and stroke his brown fur. The mule seemed to like them and made a funny sound with his nose. Sam and Jen giggled.

"I want to ride the mule, dad," Jen said.

"Me too, me too," Sam said.

Dad looked at the mule and shook his head. "I don't think that's a good idea, kids. The mule is not used to having people on his back. He might get scared and run away or kick. He is an enormous mule, you know. He could hurt you or himself."

But Sam and Jen did not listen to dad. They waited until he was busy talking to the farmer and climbed on the mule's back. They held on to his mane and shouted, "Giddy up, mule!"

The mule did not like that at all. He felt two small people on his back and heard loud noises in his ears. He got scared and angry. He started to run and buck and shake his head. He wanted to get rid of the twins.

Sam and Jen screamed and cried. They were scared and sorry. They wished they had listened to dad. They tried to hold on to the mule, but he was too fast and wild. They fell off the mule and landed on the grass.

Dad heard the commotion and ran to the twins. He checked them for injuries and hugged them. He was relieved that they were not hurt, but he was also angry and disappointed. He scolded them for disobeying him and putting themselves in danger.

The mule calmed down and came back to the fence. He looked at the twins with his big eyes and snorted. He was glad that they were off his back, but he also felt sorry for them. He nudged them with his nose and licked their faces. He forgave them.

Sam and Jen apologized to the mule and to dad. They learned their lesson. They promised to never ride the mule again without permission and to respect his feelings. They gave him some carrots and apples and thanked him for being a good mule. They hugged him and said goodbye.

The end.

Sara and Ben like to play with cards. They have many cards with animals on them. They try to find two cards that are the same. They call this game "match".

One day, they go to the park with Mom. They see a big sign with a map. The map shows many paths in the park. Sara and Ben want to explore the paths. They ask Mom if they can play "match" with the map.

Mom says yes, but they have to stay close to her. She gives them each a card with a picture of a path. She tells them to look at the map and find the path that matches their card. Then they can walk on that path with her.

Sara and Ben are excited. They look at their cards and the map. Sara has a card with a path that goes around a pond. She finds the path on the map. She points to it and says, "Look, Mom, I found my match!"

Ben has a card with a path that goes through a forest. He looks at the map, but he cannot find the path. He sees many paths, but none of them look like his card. He feels confused. He says, "Mom, I can't find my match. Where is it?"

Mom looks at Ben's card and the map. She smiles and says, "Ben, your card is special. It shows a hidden path that is not on the map. You have to look for a sign with a squirrel on it. That is the entrance to the forest path. Do you want to try to find it?"

Ben nods and says, "Yes, Mom, I want to find the hidden path. That sounds fun!" He looks around and sees a sign with a squirrel on it. He runs to it and says, "I found it, Mom, I found it!"

Mom, Sara and Ben follow the sign and enter the forest path. They see many trees and animals. They enjoy their walk and their game of "match".

One day, there was a cherry tree in the garden. It had lots of beautiful cherries. Each one was shiny and red.

The gardener wanted to make them even shinier, so she got a cloth and began to polish the cherries. She moved the cloth up and down and round and round, until the cherries were so shiny that she could see her face in them!

Then, a foolish bird came and tried to eat the cherries. But he was so silly that he didn't notice how shiny they were. So, he hurt his beak on them.

The gardener thought this was very funny. She laughed and laughed and told the bird to be more careful. The bird soon flew away, better off for the lesson!

Once upon a time, there was a big bird named Billy. Billy loved to soar high in the sky. He liked to see the trees, the animals, and the water from up high.

One day, Billy saw a little girl named Sally playing by the shore. Sally was making a sandcastle, but she looked sad. Billy flew down to Sally and said, "Why are you sad?"

Sally looked at Billy and said, "I want to make a silly sandcastle, but I can't do it alone." Billy wanted to help Sally, so he used his big wings to help her build the silliest sandcastle ever.

Together, they made a sandcastle with a funny face and wavy walls. When they were done, Sally was happy and thanked Billy for his help. From that day on, Billy and Sally became the best of friends, and they always played together by the shore.

Once upon a time, there was a shy bunny named Jack. Jack lived in a big forest with his friends, the birds and the squirrels. One day, Jack received a letter from his grandma inviting him to a party. Jack was so excited! He had never been to a party before.

Jack went to his friends and asked for their help to get ready for the party. The birds helped Jack pick out a nice outfit and the squirrels helped him practice his dance moves. When Jack arrived at the party, he saw all of his family and friends. He was so happy to be a part of the celebration.

At the end of the party, Jack's grandma gave him a big hug and said, "I'm so glad you came to the party, Jack. You are such a special part of our family." Jack felt so loved and happy. From that day on, Jack was no longer shy. He knew that he was a special part of his family and friends' lives.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat cookies and candy, but her mom said she needed to eat healthy food too. One day, Lily's mom brought home a filet of fish for dinner. Lily had never tried fish before, but her mom said it was delicious.

Lily looked at the fish and thought it was enormous! She didn't think she could eat it all. But her mom said she could just eat a little bit and save the rest for later. Lily took a bite and was surprised that it tasted really good! She ate more and more until she had finished the whole filet.

Later that night, Lily had a dream about a giant fish that was even more enormous than the one she had eaten for dinner. She was scared at first, but then remembered how yummy the fish had been. She reached out and took a bite of the giant fish, and it was just as delicious as the one she had eaten earlier. From then on, Lily wasn't afraid to try new foods and always remembered to take just a little bite at first.

Once upon a time there was a little rabbit. She was small and soft and she had big ears. One day she wanted to go to the park to find some yummy grass to eat. She hopped and hopped until she found a park. It was a pretty park with lots of green grass. 

The little rabbit was so excited! She started to eat the grass. It was so nice and crunchy and sweet. But then she heard something move in the grass. It was a little frog! He was hopping around looking for food too. The rabbit said "Hello! Would you like some of my grass?" 

The frog said yes, so the rabbit gave him some grass. The frog was so thankful. He said "Thank you for the supply of grass". The frog hugged the rabbit and went on his way.

The rabbit went back to eating her grass. But the grass was too tight! The rabbit pulled and pulled, but the grass wouldn't come up. Then the frog returned with an idea! He said "Here, hold on tight and I will pull up the grass for you". 

The rabbit held on tight, and the frog pulled the grass up. The rabbit was so happy because now she could eat the grass easily.

from typing import Iterator

from ...base import BaseExtractor, DatasetMeta, RawTriple
from ...paths import default_raw_dir
from .model import META, PROPERTIES, WikidataPropertyDump, WikidataRawLayout


def _is_qid_fallback(label: str) -> bool:
    return len(label) > 1 and label.startswith("Q") and label[1:].isdigit()


class WikidataExtractor(BaseExtractor):
    def meta(self) -> DatasetMeta:
        return META

    def extract(self, config: dict) -> Iterator[RawTriple]:
        base_raw = Path(config.get("raw_dir") or default_raw_dir())
        layout = WikidataRawLayout(raw_dir=base_raw / META.category / META.name)

        for prop in PROPERTIES:
            path = layout.property_path(prop.pid)
            if not path.exists():
                continue

            try:
                raw = json.loads(path.read_text())
                dump = WikidataPropertyDump.model_validate(raw)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[wikidata] {prop.pid} unreadable: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
                continue

            seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
            for row in dump.bindings:
                a = row.get("itemLabel", {}).get("value", "").strip()
                b = row.get("valueLabel", {}).get("value", "").strip()
                if not a or not b or a == b:
                    continue
                if _is_qid_fallback(a) or _is_qid_fallback(b):
                    continue
                key = (a, b)
                if key in seen:
                    continue
                seen.add(key)
                yield RawTriple(
                    subject=a,
                    relation=prop.relation,
                    object=b,
                    provenance=f"wikidata:{prop.pid}",
                )

"""Hand-curated country reference data.

This dataset has no external source to download — the tables in ``_data.py``
are the authoritative copy. Edit in place to extend coverage. The
downloader is a no-op that always reports ready; the extractor walks the
tables and yields one triple per country-field pair.

Sources combined in ``_data.py``:

- **ISO 3166-1** — alpha-2, alpha-3, and numeric country codes
- **UN M49**    — region and subregion classification
- **ISO 4217**  — currency codes
- **E.164**     — international calling codes
- **IANA**      — country-code top-level domains
- **UN member list** — membership flag
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from ...base import DatasetMeta, RawLayout
from ._data import COUNTRIES


META = DatasetMeta(
    name="countries",
    category="knowledge",
    version="2026-04",
    license="public domain (hand-curated from ISO/UN/IANA sources)",
    url="https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html",
    layer_band="knowledge",
    description="ISO 3166 + UN country reference: codes, capitals, regions, currencies, languages",
)


class CountriesRawLayout(RawLayout):
    """Countries has no raw artifacts — layout is a marker only."""

    def verify(self) -> bool:
        return True


# Mapping from dict key → relation name. Keys not in this map are not
# emitted; this makes adding / renaming a relation a one-line change.
SCALAR_RELATIONS: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    ("alpha2",        "iso_alpha2"),
    ("alpha3",        "iso_alpha3"),
    ("numeric",       "iso_numeric"),
    ("capital",       "capital"),
    ("continent",     "continent"),
    ("region",        "region"),
    ("subregion",     "subregion"),
    ("currency",      "currency"),
    ("currency_name", "currency_name"),
    ("calling_code",  "calling_code"),
    ("tld",           "tld"),
]


__all__ = ["META", "COUNTRIES", "CountriesRawLayout", "SCALAR_RELATIONS"]

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Convert a TinyModel .pt checkpoint to HF-safetensors layout.

Produces a directory consumable by `larql extract-index`:

    <output>/
      model.safetensors     # native TinyModel keys (no remap)
      config.json           # model_type="tinymodel" + arch fields
      tokenizer.json        # copied from ../../../tokenizer/v11/artifacts/

Native key layout is matched directly by `TinyModelArch` in
`larql-models/src/architectures/tinymodel.rs` — no tensor renaming.

Usage:
    uv run --project model/v11-train python \\
      model/v11-train/convert_to_safetensors.py \\
      --artifact-dir model/v11 \\
      --checkpoint model_compiled.pt \\
      --output   model/v11/safetensors
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import torch
from safetensors.torch import save_file

from tiny_model_v11 import load_config


def strip_buffers(state: dict) -> dict:
    """Drop non-parameter buffers (rope_freqs) and tied weights
    (lm_head aliases embed). Safetensors refuses to serialize memory-
    shared tensors; the HF loader reconstructs lm_head from embed
    when `tie_word_embeddings: true`."""
    dropped = {"lm_head.weight"}
    return {
        k: v for k, v in state.items()
        if not k.endswith("rope_freqs") and k not in dropped
    }


def hf_config(arch, extras: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    """HF-style config.json that detect_from_json parses into ModelConfig.

    Fields align with larql-models/src/detect.rs::parse_model_config:
      model_type, hidden_size, num_hidden_layers, intermediate_size,
      num_attention_heads, num_key_value_heads, head_dim, vocab_size,
      max_position_embeddings, rope_theta.
    """
    cfg = {
        "model_type": "tinymodel",
        "architectures": ["TinyModel"],
        "hidden_size": arch.dim,
        "num_hidden_layers": arch.n_layers,
        "intermediate_size": arch.ffn_dim,
        "num_attention_heads": arch.n_heads,
        "num_key_value_heads": arch.n_kv_heads,
        "head_dim": arch.dim // arch.n_heads,
        "vocab_size": arch.vocab_size,
        "max_position_embeddings": arch.max_seq,
        "rope_theta": 10000.0,
        "tie_word_embeddings": True,
        "torch_dtype": "float32",
    }
    if extras:
        cfg.update(extras)
    return cfg


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--artifact-dir", type=Path, required=True,
                    help="TinyModel artefact dir (contains config.json + artifacts/)")
    ap.add_argument("--checkpoint", default="model_compiled.pt",
                    help="Filename under artifact-dir/artifacts/ to convert")
    ap.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True,
                    help="Destination HF-safetensors directory")
    ap.add_argument("--tokenizer",
                    default=None,
                    help="Path to tokenizer.json (default: <repo>/tokenizer/v11/artifacts/tokenizer.json)")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    artifact_dir = args.artifact_dir.resolve()
    output = args.output.resolve()
    output.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    config = load_config(artifact_dir)
    ckpt_path = artifact_dir / "artifacts" / args.checkpoint
    print(f"[load] {ckpt_path}")
    state = torch.load(str(ckpt_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=True)
    state = strip_buffers(state)
    state = {k: v.contiguous().cpu() for k, v in state.items()}
    print(f"       {len(state)} tensors, "
          f"{sum(v.numel() for v in state.values()):,} params total")

    # Sample keys (for sanity): should start "embed.weight", "layers.0.attn.q_proj.weight", …
    sample = list(state.keys())[:4]
    print(f"[keys] e.g. {sample}")

    safetensors_path = output / "model.safetensors"
    print(f"[save] {safetensors_path}")
    save_file(state, str(safetensors_path))

    cfg = hf_config(config, extras={"tinymodel_version": config.version})
    config_path = output / "config.json"
    print(f"[save] {config_path}")
    config_path.write_text(json.dumps(cfg, indent=2) + "\n")

    # Tokenizer
    if args.tokenizer is not None:
        tok_src = Path(args.tokenizer)
    else:
        repo_root = artifact_dir.parents[1]  # <tiny-model>/
        tok_src = repo_root / "tokenizer" / "v11" / "artifacts" / "tokenizer.json"
    if tok_src.exists():
        tok_dst = output / "tokenizer.json"
        print(f"[copy] {tok_src} → {tok_dst}")
        shutil.copy(tok_src, tok_dst)
    else:
        print(f"[warn] tokenizer.json not found at {tok_src}; "
              "extract-index will require one alongside the model")

    print(f"\n[done] HF-safetensors layout at: {output}")
    print("Next:")
    print(f"  larql extract-index {output} --output {output.parent / 'vindex'} --level all")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Reads the FrameNet corpus and yields raw triples.

Emits:

* ``evokes_frame``  (word, frame_name)     — lexical unit to frame
* ``frame_parent``  (parent_frame, child)  — inter-frame hierarchy
* ``frame_element`` (frame_name, element)  — each frame's roles
* ``frame_lexicon`` (frame_name, word)     — frame back to its lemmas
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Iterator

from ...base import BaseExtractor, DatasetMeta, RawTriple
from ...paths import default_raw_dir, setup_nltk_data_dir
from .model import META


class FrameNetExtractor(BaseExtractor):
    def meta(self) -> DatasetMeta:
        return META

    def extract(self, config: dict) -> Iterator[RawTriple]:
        raw_dir = Path(config.get("raw_dir") or default_raw_dir())
        setup_nltk_data_dir(raw_dir)
        from nltk.corpus import framenet as fn  # type: ignore

        for frame in fn.frames():
            frame_name = frame.name
            for lu in frame.lexUnit.values():
                word = lu.name.split(".")[0]
                yield RawTriple(
                    subject=word,
                    relation="evokes_frame",
                    object=frame_name,
                    provenance=frame_name,
                )
                yield RawTriple(
                    subject=frame_name,
                    relation="frame_lexicon",
                    object=word,
                    provenance=frame_name,
                )
            for fe in frame.FE.values():
                yield RawTriple(
                    subject=frame_name,
                    relation="frame_element",
                    object=fe.name,
                    provenance=f"{frame_name}/{fe.coreType}",
                )

        for rel in fn.frame_relations():
            parent = getattr(rel, "superFrameName", None)
            child = getattr(rel, "subFrameName", None)
            if not parent or not child:
                continue
            rel_type = getattr(rel.type, "name", "related")
            yield RawTriple(
                subject=parent,
                relation="frame_parent",
                object=child,
                provenance=rel_type,
            )

But you have to be good dogs and follow me. And you have to bow to me when I say so. That is how you show respect to a captain."

Sam and Mia followed the captain, but they did not understand what bow meant. They thought it meant to play. So when the captain said, "Bow to me, dogs!", they jumped on him and licked his face. The captain was angry and pushed them away. He said, "No, no, no! That is not how you bow! You have to lower your head and bend your body. Like this!" He showed them how to bow.

Sam and Mia tried to copy him, but they did not like it. They thought it was boring and silly. They wanted to play and explore. They saw many things on the boat that looked fun. Sam and Mia did not listen to him. They thought he was mean and bossy. They wanted to go back to the port and play with each other. They ran to the gate and tried to open it, but it was locked. They barked and scratched, but no one came. They were trapped on the boat with the angry captain.

The captain caught them and said, "You are very naughty dogs! You have no manners and no respect. You have to learn a lesson. I will take you with me on my boat and teach you how to behave. You will not see the port or your home again until you bow to me and do what I say!"

Sam and Mia were scared and sad. They did not want to go with the captain. They wanted to go back to the port and play with each other. They wished they had listened to him and bowed to him when he said so. They realized they had made a big mistake.

Moral: Sometimes it is good to listen and follow the rules, even if you do not understand or like them. It can save you from trouble and keep you safe.

Once upon a time there was a pretty girl called Jane. Jane was very kind and generous. One day, she went for a walk in the woods. As she was walking, something strange caught her eye. Once upon a time there were two best friends named Abigail and Jack. Whenever the two of them got together, they would always play around and have so much fun.

Mia and Ben were playing with their toys in the living room. They had a lot of fun making noises and pretending to be animals. Mia had a dish that she used as a hat, and Ben had a car that he drove around the couch.

"Look at me, I'm a princess!" Mia said, putting the dish on her head.

They played and played until they felt very tired. They started to yawn and rub their eyes.

"Are you sleepy, Mia?" Ben asked.

But in the middle of the night, they heard a loud noise. It sounded like a crash. They opened their eyes and saw a shadow in the living room. They felt scared and curious.

"Who is that, Mia?" Ben whispered.

They got out of their beds and tiptoed to the living room. They peeked around the corner and saw a big raccoon. The raccoon had knocked over the trash can and was looking for food. It had found Mia's dish and was trying to eat it.

"Hey, that's my dish!" Mia said, forgetting to be quiet.

The raccoon heard her and turned around. It saw Mia and Ben and growled. It looked angry and hungry.

"Run, Mia, run!" Ben said, grabbing her hand.

They ran back to their bedroom and slammed the door. They heard the raccoon scratching and banging on the door. They were very scared and wished they had stayed in bed.

They hugged each other and cried. They wished their mom and dad were home. They had gone out for a date and left them with a babysitter. But the babysitter had fallen asleep on the couch and did not hear the noise.

They waited and waited until the raccoon gave up and left. They heard the car pull up in the driveway and the door open. They heard their mom and dad call their names.

"Mia, Ben, are you okay?" their mom asked, opening their bedroom door.

"Mama, Papa, we're so glad you're back!" Mia and Ben said, running to hug them.

They told them what had happened and how scared they were. Their mom and dad hugged them and comforted them. They checked the living room and saw the mess. They also saw the babysitter still asleep on the couch.

They were not happy with the babysitter and decided to find a new one. They cleaned up the living room and threw away Mia's dish. They told Mia and Ben that they were sorry they had left them alone and that they loved them very much.

They tucked them back into their beds and kissed them good night. They told them to always stay in bed and be alert when they hear a strange noise. They told them to never go out of their bedroom without an adult.

Mia and Ben learned their lesson and nodded. They said they loved their mom and dad too and that they were sorry they had been naughty. They closed their eyes and fell asleep again.

They dreamed of happy things and did not hear any more noises. They slept peacefully until the morning.

Timmy was feeling lonely, so he went walking in the meadow. He was looking for a friend, but it seemed like everyone he saw was too busy to talk. Suddenly, he saw someone in the distance. Timmy grew excited and ran towards the figure.

When he reached the figure, he realised it was a young girl. He excitedly shouted, "Hi! I'm Timmy, let's be friends!" But his enthusiasm embarrassed the girl and she didn't answer. She just left, quickly walking away.

Timmy felt sad and misunderstood. He had been too distant, he should have stopped and introduced himself properly. He realised that from now on he would have to try harder to make friends. Timmy walked back home alone, feeling more alone than ever before.

Moral of the story: It is important to know when to be distant and when to be friendly. Too much distance can make people feel lonely, but pushing yourself too hard can also make people embarrassed and uncomfortable.

"""Atomic JSON triple writer with dedup."""

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Iterable

from .base import RawTriple
from .io_utils import atomic_write_json


def _dedupe(triples: Iterable[RawTriple]) -> list[list[str]]:
    seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
    out: list[list[str]] = []
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.object)
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        out.append([t.subject, t.object])
    return out


class TripleWriter:
    def write(
        self,
        triples: Iterable[RawTriple],
        output_path: str,
        relation: str,
        source: str,
        description: str,
        layer_band: str,
    ) -> int:
        pairs = _dedupe(triples)
        payload = {
            "source": source,
            "relation": relation,
            "description": description,
            "layer_band": layer_band,
            "extracted": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "pair_count": len(pairs),
            "pairs": pairs,
        }
        atomic_write_json(Path(output_path), payload)
        return len(pairs)

"""Fetches the Geofabrik Great Britain PBF extract.

~2 GB streaming download with a tqdm byte-progress bar and HTTP Range
resume support (Geofabrik honours ``Range:`` so a killed download picks
up at the .tmp sibling's current size). The final file is renamed into
place atomically so a killed run never leaves a corrupted target.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from pathlib import Path

import requests
from tqdm import tqdm

from ...base import BaseDownloader, DatasetMeta
from .model import GEOFABRIK_URL, META, MIN_PBF_BYTES, OsmGbRawLayout


CHUNK_BYTES = 1024 * 1024  # 1 MiB
REQUEST_TIMEOUT = 600
USER_AGENT = "knowledge-extractor/0.3"


class OsmGbDownloader(BaseDownloader):
    def meta(self) -> DatasetMeta:
        return META

    def layout(self, raw_dir: Path) -> OsmGbRawLayout:
        return OsmGbRawLayout(raw_dir=self.raw_path(raw_dir))

    def download(self, raw_dir: Path) -> None:
        layout = self.layout(raw_dir)
        target = layout.pbf_path()
        if target.exists() and target.stat().st_size >= MIN_PBF_BYTES:
            return

        tmp = target.with_suffix(target.suffix + ".tmp")
        tmp.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        # Resume partial downloads: if a previous run died mid-transfer the
        # .tmp will still be here. Geofabrik supports Range requests.
        resume_from = tmp.stat().st_size if tmp.exists() else 0

        session = requests.Session()
        session.headers["User-Agent"] = USER_AGENT
        headers: dict[str, str] = {}
        if resume_from:
            headers["Range"] = f"bytes={resume_from}-"

        with session.get(
            GEOFABRIK_URL,
            stream=True,
            timeout=REQUEST_TIMEOUT,
            headers=headers,
        ) as r:
            # If the server can't honour the range request it sends 200 and
            # the whole file — restart the .tmp from scratch in that case.
            if resume_from and r.status_code == 200:
                resume_from = 0
            r.raise_for_status()

            content_length = int(r.headers.get("content-length", 0))
            total = (content_length + resume_from) if content_length else None

            mode = "ab" if resume_from else "wb"
            with open(tmp, mode) as f, tqdm(
                total=total,
                initial=resume_from,
                unit="B",
                unit_scale=True,
                unit_divisor=1024,
                desc="osm-gb",
                miniters=1,
            ) as bar:
                for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=CHUNK_BYTES):
                    if chunk:
                        f.write(chunk)
                        bar.update(len(chunk))

        os.replace(tmp, target)

"""Fetches the FrameNet 1.7 corpus via NLTK."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import ClassVar

from ...base import DatasetMeta, NLTKBackedDownloader, NLTKRawLayout
from .model import META, FrameNetRawLayout


class FrameNetDownloader(NLTKBackedDownloader):
    NLTK_PACKAGES: ClassVar[list[tuple[str, str]]] = FrameNetRawLayout.NLTK_PACKAGES
    LAYOUT_CLASS: ClassVar[type[NLTKRawLayout]] = FrameNetRawLayout

    def meta(self) -> DatasetMeta:
        return META

Once upon a time there was a boy named Tom. Tom was very creative. He was outside one day playing with his wheel. He pushed it back and forth with his foot. It was so much fun.

Tom wanted to do something else. He had an idea. He decided to stand on the wheel. He was a bit scared but he was also very brave. He knew he could do it.

He stood on the wheel carefully. He wobbled at first but soon he was steady. He felt like he was floating in the air. It was so cool!

Tom slid down the hill on the wheel. It was so much fun. He could go faster and faster. It was one of the most fun things he had ever done.

At the end of the day, Tom was very happy and proud of himself. He had stood on the wheel and it had been so much fun. He was so creative!

One day, a little girl named Lucy went for a walk in the forest. She liked to look at the trees and listen to the birds. While she was walking, she saw a big trunk. It was old and covered with leaves.

Lucy walked closer and said, "Hello, trunk. What are you doing here?" The trunk opened up, and a kind, compassionate voice came from inside. "Hello, Lucy. I am here to help you discover something new."

Lucy was curious and asked, "What can I discover?" The compassionate voice said, "Look inside me, and you will find a surprise." Lucy opened the trunk and found a beautiful butterfly. The butterfly flew out and landed on her hand.

Lucy smiled and said, "Thank you, trunk, for helping me discover this lovely butterfly." The trunk replied, "You're welcome, Lucy. I am always here to help you discover new things. Remember to be compassionate, just like the butterfly."

From that day on, Lucy visited the trunk often. Each time, she discovered something new and learned to be more compassionate. She shared her discoveries with her friends and family, spreading kindness and love wherever she went.

Once upon a time, there were two kids named Jane and Tom. Jane and Tom played together all the time. They dreamed of going somewhere luxurious.

One hot day, Tom said to Jane, "Let's guess where we can go for luxury." 

Jane thought for a minute. Then she said with a smile, "I think we can go to the beach for a luxury vacation. We can play in the sand and get hot there!"

Tom replied, "That won't be luxury! We need to stay in a big hotel with a pool. That is luxury!"

Jane had a different idea of luxury. She said, "No, the beach is more fun than a big hotel! We can go swimming in the ocean and find seashells too!" 

Tom and Jane looked at each other. They had to find a way to make both ideas become luxury. Then they both had the same idea at the same time. They said together, "Let's go to the beach and stay in a nice hotel too!" 

The two kids hugged each other and ran off to the beach. They had the most luxurious vacation ever!

The swing suddenly felt different and suddenly, it broke! Old Fred fell and he was so surprised. He was a bit scared, but he started laughing and sneezed one more time.

One day, a little girl named Lily was in the kitchen with her mom. They were making a cake. Lily's mom said, "We need one teaspoon of sugar." Lily looked at the big spoon and the small spoon. She knew the small one was a teaspoon.

Lily's friend, Max, came over to play. He saw the cake and said, "Wow, that looks yummy!" Lily told Max about the teaspoon. Max said, "I have a modern teaspoon at home. It is very cool." Lily wanted to see Max's modern teaspoon.

Later, Lily went to Max's house. Max showed her the modern teaspoon. It was shiny and had a button. When Max pressed the button, the teaspoon made a funny sound. They both laughed. Lily was happy she got to see the modern teaspoon. And they remembered the fun time they had making the cake.

Once there was a clumsy bear that liked to put his clothes on all by himself. Every morning he wanted to do it so much that he would jump out of bed and start to put them on. But he was clumsy and he couldn't do it quickly like the other animals. 

At first, this made the bear feel very sad. He felt like he was behind everyone else. But then one day his friend gave him some advice. He told the bear to go slow and be careful when putting his clothes on. 

He tried this new way and soon discovered that he didn't have to be so clumsy. He was thankful for his friend's advice. From then on, the bear goes slow and takes his time when putting his clothes on. As a result, the bear is actually in front of everyone else when it is time to start the day.

The moral of the story is that it is better to take your time and go slow, than it is to rush and be clumsy.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. Her name was Ruth. She had her own special bedroom. It was her favorite place. 

Ruth liked to imagine that she was a princess living in a big castle. She would sit in her big comfy chair and look out her bedroom window. 

In her imagination she would travel to many great places. She would discover new adventures on every one of her travels. 

Ruth was very brave and always successful in all of her adventures. She was so proud of her successes! 

Ruth loved spending time in her bedroom, using her imagination and going on her wonderful adventures. She was so very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a smart cat. The cat lived in a small house with a red roof. The cat loved to play on the roof all day long. 

One day, the cat saw a big ball in the sky. The ball was yellow and very bright. The cat wanted to pass the ball to its friends. So, the cat waited for the ball to come down.

When the ball came down, it was night time. The cat passed the ball to its friends, and they all played together. They had so much fun under the dark sky. At the end of the night, the cat went back to the red roof house and slept, dreaming of the next time they could play with the bright ball again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and cut paper with her scissors. One day, she was cutting a piece of paper and accidentally cut her finger. It hurt a lot and she started to cry.

Her mom came running into the room and saw what happened. She took the scissors away and said, "Scissors are sharp and can be harsh. You have to be careful when you use them."

Lily nodded and wiped away her tears. Her mom gave her a band-aid and a hug. "Don't worry, everything will be okay," her mom said with a smile.

Lily felt better and smiled back at her mom. From that day on, she was more careful when using scissors and never cut herself again.

Lily and Max were squirrels who lived in a big tree. They liked to play and run and jump on the branches. One day, they found a shiny acorn on the ground. It was brown and smooth and hard.

"Wow, look at this acorn!" Lily said. "It is so pretty. I want to keep it."

"No, I want to keep it!" Max said. "I saw it first. It is mine."

They both grabbed the acorn and pulled and tugged. They did not want to share. They did not listen to each other. They started to fight.

"Stop it, stop it!" Mama squirrel said. She came out of the tree and saw them. She was not happy. "Why are you fighting over an acorn? You have many acorns in the tree. You should be kind and share."

"But Mama, this acorn is special. It is shiny and smooth and hard. We both want it." Lily and Max said.

Mama squirrel shook her head. She took the acorn from them and held it up. "Do you know what this acorn is?" she asked.

Lily and Max looked at the acorn. They did not know.

"This acorn is a seed. A seed can grow into a new tree. But it needs water and soil and sun and time. It needs to be patient. If you plant this acorn in the ground and take care of it, one day it will sprout and grow and become a big tree. Then you will have more branches to play on and more acorns to eat. But if you keep this acorn and fight over it, it will not grow. It will stay small and hard and lonely. What do you think is better?"

Lily and Max thought about it. They looked at the acorn. They looked at each other. They felt sorry for fighting. They did not want to be selfish. They wanted to be kind and share.

"Mama, we are sorry. We do not want to keep the acorn. We want to plant it and grow a new tree. Can we do that?" Lily and Max said.

Mama squirrel smiled. She was proud of them. "Yes, you can. That is a very good idea. Come on, let's find a good spot to plant the acorn. And remember, you have to be patient. It will take a long time for the acorn to grow. But it will be worth it."

Lily and Max nodded. They followed Mama squirrel and carried the acorn. They found a good spot and dug a hole. They put the acorn in the hole and covered it with soil. They watered it and gave it a kiss. They said goodbye to the acorn and thanked it for being a seed. They hoped it would grow into a big tree.

Then they ran back to their tree and hugged each other. They were happy. They learned to share and to be patient. They learned that seeds are special and can make new life. They learned that acorns are not just for keeping. They are for growing.

Sara and Ben went to the zoo with their mom. They saw many animals, like lions, monkeys, and elephants. They liked the animals, but they wanted to see the seals. Mom said they could go to the seal pool after lunch.

They ate their sandwiches and drank their juice. Then they ran to the seal pool. They saw many seals swimming and splashing in the water. Some seals were lying on the rocks and sunning themselves. Sara and Ben clapped and laughed. They wanted to touch the seals.

One seal was very friendly. It came close to the edge of the pool and looked at Sara and Ben. It made a loud noise, like "Arf, arf, arf!" Sara and Ben thought it was saying hello. They reached out their hands to pet the seal.

But the seal did not like that. It was frightened of the children. It thought they wanted to hurt it. It opened its mouth and showed its teeth. It made a louder noise, like "Rrrr, rrrr, rrrr!" Sara and Ben were frightened too. They pulled back their hands and screamed.

Mom heard them and came quickly. She hugged them and said, "Don't worry, the seal was just scared. It didn't mean to scare you. You have to be careful with wild animals. They are not like pets. You can look at them, but you can't touch them. Do you understand?"

Sara and Ben nodded. They said they were sorry. They said they still liked the seals, but they would not try to pet them again. Mom said that was good. She said they could watch the seals for a while, and then go see some other animals. Sara and Ben smiled. They were not frightened anymore. They were happy.

Once there was a lazy butterfly. He spent all his days fluttering around in the meadow. One day he spotted a big, quiet tree. So he decided to take a rest there and when he did, his eyes closed and he began to blink.

Soon enough, the little butterfly was fast asleep. He slept so quietly that no one bothered him.

When dusk began to come, the butterfly woke up. He lazily fluttered out of the tree and started playing in the meadow again. Everywhere he fluttered he had a big, friendly smile on his face.

The next day, the butterfly blinked and flew away, no one ever knew where he went. But his friends in the meadow couldn't help but imagine what adventures he could have had in his far away destination.

Lily and Tom were friends. They liked to play outside. One day, they saw a big hill. They wanted to climb it. They walked and walked until they reached the top.

At the top, they saw a cliff. A cliff is a place where the ground stops and there is a big drop. Lily and Tom were curious. They wanted to see what was on the other side. They walked closer and closer to the edge.

But then, they heard a loud noise. It was a bird. A big bird with a long beak and sharp claws. It was angry. It did not like Lily and Tom near its nest. It flew towards them and tried to peck them.

Lily and Tom were scared. They ran away from the cliff. They ran back to the hill. They ran down the hill. They ran all the way to their home.

They were safe. They hugged each other. They were happy to return to their normal place. They decided not to go near the cliff again. They learned that some places are not for playing. They played with their toys instead. They were still friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tweet. Tweet loved to fly high in the sky and feel the wind beneath his wings. One day, while Tweet was flying, he saw a worm on the ground. The worm looked lost and scared.

Tweet wanted to be helpful, so he swooped down and picked up the worm in his beak. He flew the worm to a nearby garden and gently placed him in the soil. The worm was very grateful and thanked Tweet for being so helpful.

Later that day, as Tweet was flying again, he saw a big storm cloud in the distance. He knew he needed to get home before the storm hit. Suddenly, he remembered the worm he helped earlier. He quickly flew back to the garden and picked up the worm in his beak once again.

As the storm began to rage, Tweet soared through the wind and rain, holding the worm tightly in his beak. Finally, they made it safely back to Tweet's cozy nest. The worm was safe and sound, and Tweet was happy to have been so helpful.

Sara and Ben were playing in the kitchen. They liked to pretend they were chefs and make food with the pots and pans. Sara saw the dishwasher and wanted to open it. She thought maybe there were more things to play with inside.

"Ben, look! The dishwasher!" she said. "Can we open it?"

Ben shook his head. "No, Sara. Mom said no. The dishwasher is hot and wet. It can hurt you. Don't touch it."

But Sara did not listen. She pulled the handle and opened the door. She saw shiny plates and forks and spoons. She reached out to touch them.

"Ow!" she cried. "Hot! Hot!"

Ben ran to her and pulled her hand away. He saw a red mark on her finger. He was scared.

"Sara, are you okay? You touched the dishwasher. You hurt yourself. I told you not to touch it."

Sara was crying. She felt a big pain in her finger. She was sorry she did not listen to Ben. She hugged him and said, "I'm sorry, Ben. You were right. The dishwasher is hot and wet. It can hurt me. I won't touch it again."

Ben hugged her back and said, "It's okay, Sara. I'm sorry you hurt yourself. Let's go find Mom. She can make it better."

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. They have a lot of toys to share. But sometimes they fight over the toys.

One day, they see a big pile of leaves in the park. They want to jump in the leaves and have fun. They run to the pile and gather some leaves in their arms. They throw the leaves in the air and laugh.

But then, Ben sees a shiny red car in the pile. He likes the car. He wants to play with it. He grabs the car and runs away from Anna.

"Hey, that's not fair!" Anna shouts. "That's my car! I brought it from home! Give it back!"

"No, it's mine now!" Ben says. "I found it in the leaves! It's a new toy!"

Anna is angry and sad. She is jealous of Ben. She wants her car back. She chases Ben around the park. They yell and push each other.

A lady sees them and stops them. She is a teacher from the nearby school. She asks them what is wrong.

They tell her about the car. She looks at the car and sees a sticker on it. The sticker has Anna's name on it.

"This car belongs to Anna," she says. "She put her name on it. Ben, you have to give it back to her. It's not nice to take things that are not yours."

Ben feels ashamed. He knows he did something bad. He gives the car back to Anna. He says he is sorry.

Anna is happy to have her car back. She says she forgives Ben. She asks him if he wants to play with the car together.

Ben nods. He says he is sorry again. He says he wants to be friends with Anna.

They hug and smile. They play with the car and the leaves. They share and take turns. They have fun.

The lady watches them and smiles. She is glad they made up. She thinks they are good friends. She hopes they will stay friends for a long time. Maybe for a year or more.

Lily was a little bird who loved to fly. She liked to soar high in the sky and feel the wind on her wings. She also liked to sing sweet songs to her friends and family.

One day, Lily smelled something yummy. She followed her nose and saw a big pie on a window sill. It was round and brown and had steam coming out of it. Lily thought it was a gift for her. She flew closer and closer to the pie.

But the pie was not for Lily. It was for Mrs. Jones, who had baked it for her husband. She saw Lily trying to take a bite of her pie and shouted, "Shoo, shoo, go away, you naughty bird!" She waved her arms and scared Lily.

Lily was so frightened that she flew away very fast. But she was not careful and hit a branch on a tree. She fell to the ground and hurt her wing. She cried and cried, because her wing was delicate and now it was broken.

Luckily, Lily's friends and family heard her cry and came to help her. They carried her to a safe place and wrapped her wing with a soft cloth. They gave her some seeds and berries to make her feel better. They told her that she should not try to eat pies that belong to people, because they are hot and dangerous. They also told her that she was a good bird and they loved her very much.

Lily learned her lesson and said sorry to Mrs. Jones. She also thanked her friends and family for taking care of her. She hoped that her wing would heal soon, so she could fly and sing again. She also hoped that Mrs. Jones would make another pie, and maybe share a little piece with her.

Once upon a time, there was an old dove. The dove had many friends. One day, the dove's friends came to visit. They wanted to have a party. 

The dove said, "Let's set up the party outside!" 

The friends agreed and they set up the party. They had cake, balloons, and toys. 

One friend said, "This is the best party ever!" 

The old dove smiled and said, "I'm glad you all had fun." 

And they all lived happily ever after. The end.
